item.title: Research
item.description: I work in theoretical astrophysics focused on the physics and phenomenology of galactic starburst winds. For numerical studies, I use the massively-parallel GPU code, cholla, on the Pitzer cluster at the Ohio Supercomputer Center to run high resolution 3-dimensional hydro simulations. Research interests: supernovae driven outflows hydrodynamic instabilities X-ray astrophysics Publications You can find my publications listed here on arXiv or sao/nasa ads. first author Nguyen, D. D., Thompson, T. A., Mass-Loading and Non-Spherical Divergence in Hot Galactic Winds: Implications for X-Ray Observations, 2021, Submitted to MNRAS Aug 2021 (Accepted Oct 2021), arXiv:2108.07343 co-author Lopez, L.

item.title: CV
item.description: My academic CV can be found here.

item.title: Contact
item.description: The best way to reach me is through email at: nguyen.1971@osu.edu. My office is M2025 in the Physics Research Building at the main campus of Ohio State University.

item.title: Hello hello, welcome.
item.description: A brief timeline: I was born in San Jose, California and moved to Gilbert, Arizona during elementary grade school. I then attended Arizona State University for my undergrad and then moved to Columbus, Ohio for the graduate phd program at the Ohio State University. I enjoy hiking at national parks and skiing. The picture above was taken at the one sad hiking destination in the flatlands of Ohio: Hocking hills. Below, is a picture I took while hiking the Iceberg Lake trail at Glacier National Park in summer 2021. Growing up in Arizona, I have been spoiled with a wide

item.title: Welcome
item.description: Hello, my name is Dustin Nguyen and I am a fourth year PhD candidate at the Ohio State University. I work with professor Todd Thompson. I have information on myself, my research, CV, and contact on the right. Happy browsing!
